sentences.english: I don't use public transportation too often.
Nowadays, I like to ride my car.
I think public transportation is a great idea, but I really like the convenience of having my own vehicle.
sentences.korean: 대중교통은 좋은 아이디어지만 자기 차의 편리함이 정말 좋거든요.

sentences.english: All the time, especially here at the university, I always use the bus or the train or walk everywhere.
I can't drive, so it's my only option really.
sentences.korean: 항상 버스나 기차를 이용하거나 어디든 걸어다녀요. 운전을 못해서 유일한 선택지예요.

sentences.english: American public transportation is few and far between.

sentences.korean: 자금이 잘 지원되지 않고, 모두 차가 있어서 대중교통이 필요 없어요.

sentences.english: Everybody has cars, so you don't really have a need for public transportation.
sentences.korean: / 모두 차가 있어서 대중교통이 필요 없다.

quiz.question: What is the main topic of this passage?
